extraction.one_line_summary: KRISTOFF Alexander won bunch sprint after fighting back from being distanced, while ACKERMAN Pascal lost red jersey after being dropped with 40km to go.
tactical_patterns: EVENEPOEL Remco attacked at 106km with only 10s GC deficit, forcing UAE/Bora to chase despite sprint finish.
LUTSENKO Alexey counterattacked with HIRSCHI Marc inside 18km, disrupting sprint team organization before being caught.
UAE Emirates prioritized chasing Evenepoel over protecting Ackerman's red jersey, revealing stage win hierarchy over GC jersey.

rider_signals.ACKERMAN Pascal: negative: Dropped with 40km remaining and lost red jersey despite team control, suggesting vulnerability on even moderate terrain.
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: Solo attack from 106km out forced major teams to commit despite sprint finish, demonstrating tactical leverage beyond climbing ability.
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: positive: Regained contact after being distanced and still won sprint, showing exceptional recovery capacity mid-race.
rider_signals.LUTSENKO Alexey: neutral: Late attack inside 18km with Hirschi disrupted sprint trains but ultimately unsuccessful.
rider_signals.HIRSCHI Marc: neutral: Collaborated with Lutsenko on late attack attempt, showing willingness to gamble rather than sprint.
